title: Digest does not match error when pulling image
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Pulling the Fedora 32 toolbox image fails with:

```
$ Digest did not match, expected sha256:037a20ccce44f4a50af27f659c9572bba253abf1494f7e3f843d13c5c3910d63, got sha256:e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman pull registry.fedoraproject.org/f32/fedora-toolbox:32


**Describe the results you received:**

```
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/f32/fedora-toolbox:32...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 037a20ccce44 [--------------------------------------] 0.0b / 67.6MiB
Copying blob 03c837e31708 done  
  Digest did not match, expected sha256:037a20ccce44f4a50af27f659c9572bba253abf1494f7e3f843d13c5c3910d63, got sha256:e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855
Error: error pulling image "registry.fedoraproject.org/f32/fedora-toolbox:32": unable to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/f32/fedora-toolbox:32: unable to pull image: Error reading blob sha256:037a20ccce44f4a50af27f659c9572bba253abf1494f7e3f843d13c5c3910d63: Digest did not match, expected sha256:037a20ccce44f4a50af27f659c9572bba253abf1494f7e3f843d13c5c3910d63, got sha256:e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855
```

Verbose pull here: https://paste.centos.org/view/faa7dcdc

**Describe the results you expected:**

Pull succeeds

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.9.3
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14.2
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


```
podman-1.9.3-1.fc32.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Fedora 32 Silverblue

comments.body: @mtrmac PTAL. This reproduces on my machine. I suspect the registry is serving a malformed blob, but presumably Docker does similar validation to what we do and it succeeds.

Pulling https://registry.fedoraproject.org/v2/f32/fedora-toolbox/blobs/sha256:03c837e31708e15035b6c6f9a7a4b78b64f6bc10e6daec01684c077655becf95 down does give me a file with a SHA256 of 03c837e31708e15035b6c6f9a7a4b78b64f6bc10e6daec01684c077655becf95 so the registry seems sane. Not sure what's going on here.

e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855 is a sha256sum of an empty file; is it possible it was a transient error? Right now, `skopeo --override-os linux --insecure-policy copy --debug 'docker://registry.fedoraproject.org/f32/fedora-toolbox:32' dir:t` works for me.

@mheon Does Skopeo ^^^ work for you?

@mtrmac No - fails on two separate machines, one on the RH VPN and one not.

Seems likely that this is registry trouble given what we're seeing here, though. `wget` retrieves the correct blob, but Skopeo (and anything else using c/image) does not.
Or maybe a Go version difference? For me, both HEAD Skopeo with `go version go1.13.10 darwin/amd64` and a very old Skopeo build with `go version go1.10.8 linux/amd64` works.
@mtrmac I'm talking with the Fedora admins now, and it sounds like the path we're being handed by the registry is a redirect - any chance we don't handle that properly?

From `wget`:
```
HTTP request sent, awaiting response... 302 Found
Location: https://cdn.registry.fedoraproject.org/v2/f32/fedora-toolbox/blobs/sha256:03c837e31708e15035b6c6f9a7a4b78b64f6bc10e6daec01684c077655becf95 [following]
```
There’s a known behavior difference from Docker (~by definition a bug) in handling _credentials_ when following redirects, but redirects should be followed in principle.

@mtrmac Tracing further, it appears that the Fedora CDN may have cached bad values for certain files - likely explains the difference in the results we were seeing as we were hitting different CDN nodes. Talking with the Fedora admins to have the cache invalidated now.
Going to close this - cache invalidation seems to have resolved matters.
